title: Introduction to Codex
summary: A first-pass lesson on how Arcium is organized, how to read its records, and why Arcium is framed as confidential execution infrastructure.

bodySections.title: Orientation
bodySections.body:
Arcium Codex is a guided reading surface, not just a static archive. Each lesson is meant to turn protocol terminology into a more legible system map.

The shortest useful framing for Arcium is that it lets applications compute on sensitive inputs without forcing a single operator to see all of the underlying data.

bodySections.title: Why It Matters
bodySections.body:
That framing matters because the rest of the ecosystem depends on it. Builders, glossary terms, and encyclopedia pages all become easier to reason about once confidential execution is the baseline mental model.

Use this lesson as a vocabulary anchor before diving into the more technical modules.

quizQuestions.prompt: In one sentence, what problem does Arcium solve?
What is the primary purpose of the Codex?
choices: To organize protocol knowledge into an explorable learning surface
To run all production smart contracts

quizQuestions.prompt: Describe the main idea you want to keep in mind before opening the next lesson.
